title: Denny Delk

'''Denny Delk''' is an American actor who has voiced different ''[[Star Wars]]'' and [[LucasArts]] characters.

==Biography==
In [[1986]], he replaced [[Jim Henshaw]] as [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] for the [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] TV series.

He also returned to ''Star Wars'' with ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'', though not in the main roles: He voiced nameless characters (a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]], a dying Rebel later identified as [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Toka]]) and the narrator.

Delk voiced a Red Shirt in ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]''. However, [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] allowed Delk to become the villain, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin]], as well as a major supporting character, the [[Priest (Secret Order of the Emperor)|Priest]].

During [[1997]], Delk voiced Mutant [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]] [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]] and [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''.

In [[1998]] Delk voiced [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]], Intercom Voice and Trench Officer in ''[[Star Wars Trilogy Arcade]]''.

Delk would not return to ''Star Wars'' until [[1999]], where he would reprise Zaarin in ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''. He showed his versatility by playing different Rebel characters, including a pilot, an officer and a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]].

In [[2000]] he voiced a Slingshot Announcer in ''[[Star Wars: Early Learning Activity Center]]''.

Delk worked as the narrator for the [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (series)|Rogue Squadron series]] (as well as a commander and a wingman in its [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike|final installment]]). He also narrated ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' ([[2006]]) and voiced the training officer in [[Star Wars: Battlefront II|''Battlefront II'']] ([[2005]]).
